comparison.prompt_review_output: implemented as reviewer_gate plus boxue_final dependency gates; Boxue can add revision tasks via the task system
comparison.prompt_synthesize: implemented as bianlun_synthesis, paperwriter_draft, and boxue_final synthesis tasks
comparison.prompt_adjust_plan: implemented operationally by creating additional tasks or new delegation plans after reviewing outputs
comparison.prompt_finalize: implemented as boxue_final decision task, not as specialist execution

stronger_than_old_pipeline_tasks: role-specific tasks instead of generic phase tasks
risk constraints embedded in every task
optional teammate spawning for unblocked specialist work

tasks.title: Rank high-value knowledge gaps
acceptance_criteria: Every reported gap has supporting references or is flagged as ungrounded

tasks.title: Generate and evolve gap-grounded hypotheses
acceptance_criteria: Each hypothesis links to a gap_id
Each hypothesis contains mechanism and falsification condition

tasks.title: Run Socratic critique
acceptance_criteria: At least one counterexample per hypothesis

tasks.title: Moderate structured debate and synthesize refined hypothesis
acceptance_criteria: Arguments are separated into factual vs conceptual disagreements
Refined hypothesis lists improvements from critique

tasks.title: Audit mechanism fidelity
acceptance_criteria: All three verification layers are addressed
High-risk mechanisms are routed to revision or human review

evidence_type_annotations.claim: molecular anchoring approach

a molecular anchoring approach to restrict the interfacial reactivity of free ether solvents in diluted electrolytes, enhancing the stability of nickel-rich cathodes and mitigating exothermic processes under thermal abuse conditions
Herein, we propose a molecular anchoring approach to restrict the interfacial reactivity of free ether solvents in diluted electrolytes.
Full text excerpt:

The typical high concentration electrolyte approach relies on nearly saturated Li+ coordination to ether molecules, which is confronted with severe side reactions under high voltages ( >4.4 V) and extensive exothermic reactions between Li metal and reactive anions.
Herein, we propose a molecular anchoring approach to restrict the interfacial reactivity of free ether solvents in diluted electrolytes.

The hydrogen-bonding interactions from the anchoring solvent effectively suppress excessive ether side reactions and enhances the stability of nickel rich cathodes at 4.7 V, despite the extremely low Li+/ether molar ratio (1:9) and the absence of typical anion-derived interphase.
Here, the authors design a molecular anchoring dilute electrolyte via intermolecular hydrogen bonding with free solvents to improve the battery electrochemical and thermal stabilities.

a strategy based on dihydrogen-bonding interactions to extend the oxidative stability of ether-based electrolytes to 5.54 V without compromising ionic conductivity, enabling stable cycling at 4.5 V in lithium metal full cells

Abstract: High-voltage lithium metal batteries require electrolytes that simultaneously combine oxidative stability with Li metal compatibility, posing a major challenge for conventional ether-based systems, which are typically limited to voltages below 4.0 V. Although conventional electrolyte engin...[truncated]
Full text excerpt:
Herein, we propose a strategy based on dihydrogen-bonding interactions by introducing 0.05 M LiBH₄ into conventional ether-based electrolytes to construct a dihydrogen-bonded electrolyte.
As a result, the oxidative stability of the electrolyte is extended to 5.54 V without compromising ionic conductivity (>16 mS cm-1/30 °C).

references: Qianqian Liu et al. (2022) An Inorganic‐Dominate Molecular Diluent Enables Safe Localized High Concentration Electrolyte for High‐Voltage Lithium‐Metal Batteries doi:10.1002/adfm.202209725

evidence_type_annotations.claim: localized high-concentration electrolyte with PFPN as a nonflammable diluent
development of a safe, high-performance electrolyte for high-voltage lithium-metal batteries using an inorganic-dominant molecular diluent
This study highlights the important role of diluent in tailoring electrode/electrolyte interphases and provides a new strategy for designing high‐safety electrolytes toward high‐voltage LMBs.
Full text excerpt:
The inorganic‐dominate diluent improves the safety of the organic electrolyte, and helps to construct robust passivation interphases on both electrodes.
Specifically, PFPN accelerates the complete reduction of anions, leading to a stable anion‐derived interphase layer on Li anode.
As a result, PFPN‐based electrolyte prolongs the cycling life of LMBs based on high‐voltage LiNi0.6Co0.2Mn0.2 (NCM622) and LiNi0.8Co0.1Mn0.1 (NCM811) cathodes.

method_scenario_benchmark_triples.benchmark: high li metal compatibility but low oxidation stability
references: Zhuangzhuang Cui et al. (2024) Molecular anchoring of free solvents for high-voltage and high-safety lithium metal batteries doi:10.1038/s41467-024-46186-y

evidence_type_annotations.claim: molecular anchoring approach

a molecular anchoring approach to restrict the interfacial reactivity of free ether solvents in diluted electrolytes, enhancing the stability of nickel-rich cathodes and mitigating exothermic processes under thermal abuse conditions
Herein, we propose a molecular anchoring approach to restrict the interfacial reactivity of free ether solvents in diluted electrolytes.
Full text excerpt:

The typical high concentration electrolyte approach relies on nearly saturated Li+ coordination to ether molecules, which is confronted with severe side reactions under high voltages ( >4.4 V) and extensive exothermic reactions between Li metal and reactive anions.
Herein, we propose a molecular anchoring approach to restrict the interfacial reactivity of free ether solvents in diluted electrolytes.

The hydrogen-bonding interactions from the anchoring solvent effectively suppress excessive ether side reactions and enhances the stability of nickel rich cathodes at 4.7 V, despite the extremely low Li+/ether molar ratio (1:9) and the absence of typical anion-derived interphase.
Here, the authors design a molecular anchoring dilute electrolyte via intermolecular hydrogen bonding with free solvents to improve the battery electrochemical and thermal stabilities.

references: Qianqian Liu et al. (2022) An Inorganic‐Dominate Molecular Diluent Enables Safe Localized High Concentration Electrolyte for High‐Voltage Lithium‐Metal Batteries doi:10.1002/adfm.202209725

evidence_type_annotations.claim: localized high-concentration electrolyte with PFPN as a nonflammable diluent
development of a safe, high-performance electrolyte for high-voltage lithium-metal batteries using an inorganic-dominant molecular diluent
This study highlights the important role of diluent in tailoring electrode/electrolyte interphases and provides a new strategy for designing high‐safety electrolytes toward high‐voltage LMBs.
Full text excerpt:
The inorganic‐dominate diluent improves the safety of the organic electrolyte, and helps to construct robust passivation interphases on both electrodes.
Specifically, PFPN accelerates the complete reduction of anions, leading to a stable anion‐derived interphase layer on Li anode.
As a result, PFPN‐based electrolyte prolongs the cycling life of LMBs based on high‐voltage LiNi0.6Co0.2Mn0.2 (NCM622) and LiNi0.8Co0.1Mn0.1 (NCM811) cathodes.

evidence_type_annotations.claim: hydrofluoric acid-removable additive (DMPATMB) as an electrolyte additive
a novel insight into high-voltage electrolyte additives for LMBs, improving cycling life and capacity stability
However, their cycling life is seriously affected by unstable electrolyte/electrode interfaces and capacity instability at high voltage.

references: Michael Dato et al. (2024) Sulfur Solutions: Advancing High Voltage and High Energy Lithium Batteries with Organosulfur Electrolytes doi:10.1002/aenm.202303794

evidence_type_annotations.claim: deliberate design of electrolyte compositions
comprehensive overview of organosulfur electrolyte components for high-energy and high-voltage lithium batteries, highlighting their advantages and commercial availability

Abstract: Achieving energy densities exceeding 350 Wh kg−1 while operating at elevated voltages (>4.5 V vs Li/Li+) is attainable through judicious selection of electrochemical pairs at the cathode and anode. However, current state‐of‐the‐art electrolytes exhibit limited stability when exposed to sys...[truncated]

This limitation contributes to the degradation of electrode materials and raises critical safety concerns, impeding the commercialization of such systems.

idea_json.title: The conflicting claims in high-voltage lithium metal batteries are retested under matched voltage conditions
idea_json.hypothesis: If the conflicting claims in high-voltage lithium metal batteries are retested under matched voltage conditions, then cycle life will reveal which mechanism is valid and where the disagreement boundary lies: the condition where the competing explanations diverge.
idea_json.abstract: This proposal addresses the PaperGraph gap 'Potential conclusion conflict: high-voltage lithium metal batteries contains opposing claims: Full text excerpt: development of high-concentration ether electrolytes that stabilize cathodes and preserve structural integrity under high voltages, enabling stable battery cycli...[truncated] vs Full text excerpt: development of a non-corrosive asymmetric lithium salt (LiFAS) that enhances the cycle life of lithium metal batteries under high-temperature and high-voltage co...[truncated].'. It hypothesizes a testable mechanism: Concrete mechanism chain: (1) method capability: high contributes through a specified operation that must be mapped to observable inputs and outputs before validation; (2) scenario target: in high-voltage lithium metal batteries, the affected process is the scenario-specific measurable process named by the project evidence; (3) measurable consequence: the bridge must produce a preregistered change in cycle life. The method's input requirements are broad or not clearly specified; the experiment must make them explicit. No explicit bridge concept is currently visible; this must be treated as a human-review assumption rather than a validated mechanism. The decisive prediction is that this concrete bridge, not a generic representation change, will alter cycle life; if the bridge data or causal link is absent, the hypothesis should fail rather than be reinterpreted post hoc. MingLi treats this as a structural mutation rather than a surface rephrasing. The study is designed to be falsifiable through Build a minimal benchmark for high-voltage lithium metal batteries; compare high against canonical baselines; measure cycle life; include negative controls, ablations, and failure-mode analysis.
idea_json.related_work: Grounding evidence comes from: Xiaodi Ren et al. (2019) High-Concentration Ether Electrolytes for Stable High-Voltage Lithium Metal Batteries doi:10.1021/ACSENERGYLETT.9B00381, Zhi Liu et al. (2025) Non-corrosive asymmetric fluorinated aryl sulfonimide lithium salt for high-temperature and high-voltage lithium metal batteries doi:10.1038/s41467-025-64737-9. The proposal differs by testing the mapped gap directly with explicit baselines, ablations, and failure-mode criteria.

socratic_question_reports.thought: DuZhi generated structured Socratic questions targeting definitions, constraints, causal links, evidence gaps, and counterexamples.

questions.question: Can you rewrite the hypothesis as an explicit input -> mechanism -> output chain with evidence for each arrow?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: A single broad sentence hides missing causal links and prevents targeted revision.
questions.required_revision: Provide a three-to-five-step causal chain and cite or label the evidence for each link.

questions.question: For the evidence-derived terms high-concentration ether electrolytes, high-voltage lithium metal batteries, capacity retention, Li consumption, stable battery cycling, molecular anchoring approach, which exact term sits at the intervention, mediator, and output positions of the causal chain?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Domain-targeted causal probing forces MingLi to connect the hypothesis to field-specific entities without hardcoding the field.
questions.required_revision: Rewrite the causal chain using at least two evidence-derived terms and mark unsupported links as assumptions.

questions.question: What is the nearest domain-standard baseline or negative control that would make the claim nontrivial?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Without a baseline, the hypothesis cannot distinguish genuine mechanism from general performance drift.
questions.required_revision: Name at least one domain-standard baseline and one failure-mode or negative control.

questions.question: Which part of the hypothesis is directly measurable, and which part is inferred from those measurements?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: AHOIS-style clarification requires separating observables from inferred mechanisms before testing.
questions.required_revision: Add explicit observables, inferred constructs, and the mapping between them.

questions.question: Under what validity regime is the mechanism expected to hold, and where should it fail?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Unstated boundary conditions are a common CAWM risk under regime shift.
questions.required_revision: Add explicit assumptions, validity range, and at least one expected failure condition.

questions.question: Which sentence or result in the imported PaperGraph evidence grounds the strongest mechanistic premise?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: ARIS-style evidence gates require claim-to-source traceability, not just thematic similarity.
questions.required_revision: Map each central premise to a PaperGraph citation or mark it as speculative.

questions.question: The evidence repeatedly mentions high-concentration ether electrolytes, high-voltage lithium metal batteries, capacity retention, Li consumption, stable battery cycling, molecular anchoring approach, high li metal compatibility but low oxidation stability; which of these domain-specific constraints is actually required for the mechanism to hold?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: A strong critique should test the hypothesis against the concrete variables found in the retrieved literature, not only generic stress tests.
questions.required_revision: Name the required evidence-derived constraint, its allowed range or qualitative regime, and how it will be monitored.

questions.question: What data, instrument, simulation, or experimental platform can actually observe the claimed causal step?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: A hypothesis can be conceptually attractive but infeasible if the decisive mechanism is not observable.
questions.required_revision: Specify the observation platform and feasibility constraint for the decisive causal link.

questions.question: What outcome should occur if data_distribution: training/reference distribution -> out-of-distribution or low-data setting, and would that falsify the mechanism or only weaken it?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Counterexamples reveal whether the mechanism has real explanatory content across regimes.
questions.required_revision: Add predicted behavior under this shifted condition and define pass/fail interpretation.

questions.question: What outcome should occur if noise_level: nominal signal quality -> high-noise or missing-data condition, and would that falsify the mechanism or only weaken it?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Counterexamples reveal whether the mechanism has real explanatory content across regimes.
